title: Waves and Optics
description: Transverse and longitudinal waves, the wave equation and polarisation, superposition and standing waves, two-source interference and diffraction gratings, and refraction with total internal reflection.
objectives: Distinguish transverse and longitudinal waves and describe wave properties (amplitude, wavelength, frequency, period, speed)
Apply the wave equation v = fλ and explain polarisation as evidence that a wave is transverse
Apply the principle of superposition to explain standing waves on a string, including nodes, antinodes, harmonics and resonance
Explain path difference and coherence, and use two-source interference (λ = ax/D) and the diffraction grating equation (d sinθ = nλ)
Apply Snell's law (n = sinθ1/sinθ2) and the critical angle condition (sinC = 1/n) to refraction and total internal reflection

lessons.title: Wave properties, the wave equation and standing waves
